title: X-tra Romantic Mexican Butternut Squash Tagine
description: A funky and romantic Mexican dish featuring butternut squash, cooked in a tagine and served on a wooden board, perfect for a special occasion. This jain-friendly recipe is a unique blend of flavors and textures.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the butternut squash with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper until coated.
Spread the squash on a baking sheet and roast for 30 minutes, or until tender.
In a large tagine or Dutch oven, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, paprika, and chili powder and cook for 1 minute.
Add the roasted butternut squash, vegetable broth, and cooked onion mixture to the tagine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh cilantro, on a wooden board.
